source_pr.title: executor:return warning when load data meet no partition for incompatiable mysql,but err and abort
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #21966 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:
load data into partition table shouldn't return no partition error
### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:
when load data meet no partition, return toBeCheckedRow with err,but err returned directly in  function getKeysNeedCheckOneRow, file batch_checked.go and  append warning when noPartitionErr is not nil in batchCheckAndInsert func, file insert_common.go 

How it Works:
append warning when noPartitionErr is not nil in batchCheckAndInsert func, file insert_common.go 

### Related changes

- PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test
- Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- No code

Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more CPU
    - Consumes more MEM
- Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->
- When load data meet no partition in getPartitionByRow func, it return err directly.but should return warning for incompatiable mysql.

symptoms: rows = dbt.mustQuery("select id,name from t order by id") (server/server_test.go)
failure_signature: rows = dbt.mustQuery("select id,name from t order by id") (server/server_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR changes `LOAD DATA` behavior for partition tables (no-partition case becomes a warning instead of aborting) and updates server tests accordingly (e.g., checking `show warnings` and expected rows). The cited query already has `ORDER BY`, and the case lacks a concrete flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization mechanism, so we conservatively mark it as needing more evidence.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: evidence: rows = dbt.mustQuery("select id,name from t order by id") (server/server_test.go)

files: executor/batch_checker.go, executor/executor.go, executor/insert_common.go, executor/replace.go, executor/write_test.go, server/server_test.go, table/tables/partition.go, table/tables/partition_test.go
